game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Die drei ??? – Chamäleonbande:** Players engage in a narrative-driven detective adventure, primarily interacting with the environment and characters by tapping on hotspots to examine objects, collect clues, and initiate dialogue. The game progresses through a series of distinct locations (e.g., 5-7 per chapter), where players must gather specific clues (e.g., 3-5 pieces of evidence per scene) and interview suspects by selecting dialogue options from a branching tree, with certain choices unlocking new information or paths. Interspersed throughout the investigation are mini-puzzles, such as deciphering codes, assembling torn documents, or matching fingerprints, which require logical deduction or pattern recognition to solve and unlock further story segments. The main objective is to piece together the mystery of the "Chameleon Gang" by accumulating enough evidence and making correct deductions, which are tracked in a case file UI, leading to the identification and apprehension of the culprits. The game concludes successfully when all key clues are found, all suspects are interrogated, and the player correctly identifies the Chameleon Gang members and their motives, leading to the case's resolution, with no explicit failure state other than being unable to progress without solving a puzzle.
game_info.description: You are a young detective investigating the mysterious "Chameleon Gang" across multiple locations. Examine crime scenes, collect evidence, interview suspects, and solve puzzles to uncover the truth. Navigate through locations using arrow keys, interact with hotspots using SPACE to collect clues, and solve mini-puzzles to progress. Your goal is to gather all evidence, interrogate all suspects, and identify the gang members to solve the case!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between locations and select dialogue options
SPACE: Interact with hotspots, collect clues, and confirm selections
Z: Open/close case file to review collected evidence
SHIFT: Skip dialogue text (hold to speed up)
ENTER: Start game from main menu
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart game (returns to start screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Test basic navigation and interaction mechanics. Verify that the player can move between locations, interact with hotspots, and collect clues without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through all available locations systematically, interact with every visible hotspot, and attempt to collect clues. Move in a pattern that covers all areas of each location.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The player should successfully visit all locations, interact with all hotspots, and collect at least some clues. No crashes or stuck states should occur during navigation and interaction.

TEST_2.test_description: Test complete playthrough to win the game. Validate that all win conditions can be achieved: collecting all clues, interrogating all suspects, solving all puzzles, and correctly identifying the Chameleon Gang.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow optimal path through all locations, collect all evidence systematically, solve all puzzles using correct patterns, interrogate all suspects with optimal dialogue choices, and make correct final deduction about gang members.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Game should reach GAME_OVER_WIN state after collecting all required evidence, solving all puzzles, and making the correct identification of the Chameleon Gang members. Case file should show 100% completion.

TEST_3.test_description: Test puzzle-solving mechanics. Verify that all mini-puzzles (code decryption, document assembly, fingerprint matching) can be solved and properly unlock story progression.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Navigate directly to puzzle locations, attempt to solve each puzzle type using the correct solution patterns, and verify that solving puzzles unlocks new areas or information.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All puzzles should be solvable with the correct inputs, and solving them should unlock new clues or locations. Puzzle completion should be tracked in the case file.
